title: Liverpool to honour Mohamed Salah and Andy Robertson with special farewell films after final Anfield appearance
author: The Sun Reporter
summary: LIVERPOOL, May 22: Liverpool FC will pay tribute to club legends Mohamed Salah and Andy Robertson with the release of two special original films commemorating their careers at Anfield following Sunday’s final game of the season against Brentford. The pair are set to bid farewell to the Reds after a hugely successful nine-year spell that helped usher in one of the most decorated eras in the club’s modern history. Together, Salah and Robertson played key roles in Liverpool’s triumphs in the Premier League, UEFA Champions League, FIFA Club World Cup, FA Cup, UEFA Super Cup, and two League Cups. As part of the club’s farewell celebrations, two new LFC Originals productions will premiere on All Red Video. The first documentary, “Salah: Farewell to the King”, will be released at 5pm BST on Friday and will focus on the Egyptian forward’s extraordinary journey in the famous No.11 shirt. The film features an extended and candid interview with Salah inside Liverpool’s home dressing room at Anfield, which was transformed into a private theatre setting for the production. Alongside archive footage and highlights of his iconic goals and records, Salah reflects on the defining moments of his Liverpool career and the emotions behind them. The 33-year-old is also joined on the Anfield pitch by his two daughters as they revisit some of the stadium’s most unforgettable moments together.
